entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 – Brazil Edition: La Comunidad SAP Paraguay Estuvo Ahí
entry.content:
¡Hola comunidad SAP Paraguay! En marzo de 2026 tuvimos la oportunidad de participar en el SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 – Brazil Edition, dos jornadas intensas de aprendizaje hands-on en São Paulo, Brasil. Y como siempre, Paraguay no se quedó afuera.



¿Qué es el SAP CodeJam?
Para quienes aún no lo conocen: el SAP CodeJam es un evento práctico de entre 5 y 6 horas, respaldado por SAP, donde los participantes exploran tecnologías reales en un ambiente colaborativo y sin presión. No se trata de escuchar presentaciones — se trata de hacer, experimentar y aprender juntos.

Esta edición fue parte de un roadshow por Brasil, con paradas en la oficina de SAP en São Paulo y Exed Consulting. Los instructores fueron los Developer Advocates Antonio Maradiaga y Kevin Riedelsheimer — un lujo para cualquier developer SAP.


Los dos dias ¿Qué vivimos?
✔️3 de marzo – Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding
El primer día nos introdujo a Joule Studio, la nueva herramienta dentro de SAP Build que permite extender a Joule, el asistente digital de SAP, con flujos propios.

✔️ 4 de marzo – SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes
El segundo día fue igualmente intenso. En Exed Consulting, trabajamos con SAP Build Process Automation y SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh para construir procesos disparados por eventos.

Lo mas destacado
Más allá del contenido técnico, lo que hace especial a un CodeJam es la gente. Compartir mesa con profesionales de Brasil, resolver ejercicios juntos, debatir enfoques distintos — eso no tiene precio.
Un agradecimiento especial a Kevin Riedelsheimer, Antonio Maradiaga y Antonia Zorn por la confianza y la oportunidad


SAP Community = Conexiones Reales
Eventos como este no llegan solos. Detrás de cada CodeJam hay una comunidad activa que lo hace posible: profesionales que publican en el SAP Community Blog, que comparten sus experiencias, que asisten a eventos en la región, que conectan con Developer Advocates y que demuestran que hay una comunidad con interés real.

No alcanza con querer que suceda: hay que ser parte activa. Eso significa:

  • Crear una cuenta en SAP Community y participar con preguntas, respuestas y blogs
  • Seguir y conectar con los Developer Advocates en LinkedIn y SAP Community
  • Compartir las experiencias de los eventos regionales para que Paraguay aparezca en el radar
  • Asistir a CodeJams en países vecinos y llevar de vuelta el conocimiento.

Desde la SAP Community Paraguay seguimos trabajando para que Ciudad del Este y Asunción sean paradas reales en el mapa de la comunidad SAP de la región.


¿Qué tema te gustaría ver en un CodeJam en Paraguay? ABAP Cloud, Joule Studio, SAP Build, BTP, UI5 — cuéntanos en los comentarios. Cada opinión cuenta para hacerlo realidad.

¡Sigamos construyendo comunidad! 🚀

entry.title: Old Tile Still Appearing in SAP Build Work Zone After Complete Re‑Exposure from S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with federated content from an on‑premise S/4HANA system to SAP Build Work Zone (Standard Edition).
Our authorization team has removed the old tile and assigned a new tile to the correct custom role, custom catalog, and custom group in S/4HANA.

To ensure a clean setup, I deleted the previous exposure configuration and re‑created the full setup from scratch in:

  • /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE (content exposure)
  • SAP BTP → Build Work Zone → Content Provider → Synchronize Content

Despite repeating the full process multiple times, the old tile continues to appear in Build Work Zone instead of the new one.
The authorization consultant has verified both the new role and the tile assignments several times on the S/4HANA side, and everything looks correct there — but Build Work Zone keeps federating only the old tile.

Has anyone experienced something similar?

I am trying to understand:

  • Why outdated tile information is still being federated
  • Whether additional cleanup is required on the CDM or content provider side
  • If there are any cache layers (S/4HANA, BTP, or Build Work Zone) that must be explicitly cleared
  • Whether Exposure Version 2 requires specific steps to refresh or replace previously published app IDs

Any advice, known issues, or guidance on how to force Build Work Zone to pick up the updated tile would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Pregúntame lo que quieras con SAP Champions – Serie mensual (Sesión 2)
entry.content:
Hola querida SAP Community,

Mi nombre es María Antonieta López, SAP Champion de México.

Actualmente, trabajo como Directora de Programas SAP, enfocada en programas globales de transformación SAP, implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA, arquitectura empresarial, marcos de gobernanza e innovación digital dentro del ecosistema SAP.

Como parte de los SAP Champions que lideran la iniciativa “Ask Me Anything” junto con Sebastiano Marchesini,

Me complace informarles que yo estaré liderando la edición de marzo.🚀

Así que sí…

Pueden preguntarme. LO QUE SEA.

 

Después de más de 20 años viviendo dentro de transformaciones SAP, probablemente he visto lo bueno, lo malo… y esos momentos de “¿cómo sobrevivió este sistema al go-live?” 😅

 

Durante las próximas semanas estaré respondiendo cualquier pregunta que tengan — sin filtros — solo SAP en vivo y real.

💡Podemos explorar temas como:

⚙️Implementaciones de SAP S/4HANA y programas globales de transformación – Ejecución de implementaciones end-to-end de SAP S/4HANA y programas de transformación a gran escala, permitiendo a las organizaciones modernizar sus procesos de negocio centrales, estandarizar operaciones globales y adoptar capacidades digitales de nueva generación.

🏗️ Arquitectura Empresarial – Procesos, artefactos y estrategias para la arquitectura empresarial de SAP basada en TOGAF.

🧼 Clean Core en SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public) — procesos de gestión de proyectos para implementar o crear extensiones o interfaces en tu proyecto.

(Hablando de BUILD para configuradores y su uso con Joule, APIs preconstruidas, APIs nuevas, y por supuesto nuestra plataforma como servicio SAP Integration Suite, todo dentro de nuestra querida SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)).

☁️ RISE o GROW con SAP + Gobernanza y metodología Clean Core

Desde SAP Activate hasta marcos como SAP Application Extension Methodology, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology), SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework y, por qué no… incluso SAP Business AI Framework.

Puedes preguntar sobre qué artefactos usar, cómo ejecutar los procesos o por qué tengo que hacer esta tarea... Y te diré lo que pasará si te los saltas... Muahaha! …💀 así que mis historias de terror, como el invierno… están por llegar.

🏛️ Cómocrear un SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) o brindar servicios de SAP AM (Application Management) y realmente vivir felices para siempre con SAP usando Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍 Globalización en SAP, ya sea de forma general o por país. ¡Larga vida a SAP Document & Reporting Compliance!

Y también a las extensiones DRC de nuestros partners SAP.

🧭 Roadmaps de soluciones y estrategia de transformación, etc

¿Y cualquier otra cosa? y ... ¡si no sé la respuesta! 👉 Prometo encontrar a alguien que sí la sepa… para ustedes.

 

Ya tenemos todo listo. 💬 Ahora es tu turno.

Deja tu pregunta en la sección de comentario👇 o puedes preguntar de forma anónima aquí 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions o escaneando el QR Code 

 

📅Esta edición del AMA estará abierta para preguntas hasta el 27 de marzo de 2026.

Después de esa fecha, las preguntas seleccionadas serán respondidas en un video grabado que se publicará en el canal de YouTube de la SAP Community  👉 @SAPCommunity

También se publicará un blog resumen con las preguntas y respuestas para la comunidad.

 

Cuéntame…

🤓¿Qué tema de SAP te gustaría que exploremos juntos?

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hello,
My name is Patrick Schad, and I’m a Product Manager for SAP Build. In this post, I’d like to share important updates about the evolution of SAP Build Apps, what these changes mean for you, and how we’ll support your transition to our unified platform.

 

What's happening?

As part of the continued evolution and simplification of the SAP Build portfolio, SAP Build Apps is being deprecated as a standalone product. Moving forward, customers can leverage SAP Build unified offering, that combines application development, process automation, and business site creation into a single platform that supports low-code, code-first, and generative AI capabilities. 

 

What does this mean to you?

We recognize that change is never easy, and decisions like this are made with careful consideration. We are here to help outline what it means for you and your organization and are committed to supporting you every step of the way throughout this transition.

As of 23rd of March 2026, SAP Build Apps will be retired. Please note that there is no immediate impact on existing customers. Whether you have BTPEA/PAYG/CPEA or subscription-based licensing, you will continue to have full access to SAP Build Apps for the duration of your current contract. We will honor all existing commitments, including SLAs and maintenance, and your service will remain fully supported and operational through the end of your agreement.

 

Why are we making this change and what is the path forward?

We’ve listened closely to your feedback. As part of the ongoing evolution and simplification of the SAP Build portfolio, we are focusing on a more purpose-built approach that aligns with where the market is heading and where we can best support your needs.

Going forward, customers can leverage SAP Build offering, which unifies application development, process automation, and business site creation into a single platform that supports low-code, code-first, and generative AI capabilities.

This transition aligns with SAP’s broader strategy to simplify the commercial model and deliver a more integrated development experience across both SAP and non-SAP environments. It also allows us to focus our engineering and product investments where they can drive the greatest value and impact for your organization.

 

The Transition to SAP Build

With the retirement of SAP Build Apps, SAP Build has emerged as the comprehensive tool for all your extension, automation and application development needs. Designed to bring together low-code and pro-code capabilities ranging from drag-and-drop functionalities to advanced, AI-based code generation. SAP Build allows both developers and business experts to build and extend applications and automate processes in ways that work best for their requirements, allowing for greater developer flexibility and customization, tailored to specific business requirements and needs driving efficiency like never before.

If you're currently using SAP Build Apps, now is the time to explore how SAP Build can empower your teams with its advanced capabilities. This transition ensures you have access to the latest features, and alignment with SAP’s innovation roadmap.

 

Technical Migration from SAP Build Apps Standalone Offering to SAP Build

SAP Build Apps Frontend Projects

A direct migration path for existing SAP Build Apps frontend projects into the unified SAP Build offering is not available.

As you plan your next steps, we recommend leveraging the pro-code capabilities within SAP Build to recreate and further evolve your current and future applications. Our teams are here to support you throughout this process and help identify the best approach for your specific use cases.

 

SAP Build Apps Backend Projects

To transfer existing data from SAP Build Apps backend projects into the unified SAP Build offering, we recommend a manual approach:

In your SAP Build Apps backend project, open each entity in the data browser and use the Export option to download your data as a .csv file. Repeat this step for each entity.

In your new CAP project within SAP Build, import the downloaded .csv files. (Prerequisite: ensure that a table with the relevant fields has already been created in your CAP backend.)

For detailed instructions on uploading data into your CAP backend, please refer to this guide.

Please find here some answers to the frequently asked questions:

QuestionAnswer

How long will SAP Build Apps be supported?

We support and maintain the product until the end of your contract date.

Which tool can I replace SAP Build Apps with and how can I realize my use case?

The successor SKU is the SAP Build offering that provides a comprehensive and easy way to create applications as well as automations, business sites, native mobile apps, custom SAP Joule skills and Agents.

What advantages does SAP Build offer?

SAP Build is a comprehensive tool for all your extension, automation, and application development needs. It combines low-code and pro-code capabilities, from intuitive drag-and-drop functionality to advanced AI-powered code generation.

Can I use my SAP Build Apps project from the standalone offering and import it into the SAP Build offering?

No, this is not supported. Please refer to the help portal documentation for applicable migration paths.

What happens with ongoing web application projects?

Ongoing projects should be transitioned to our SAP Build offering. If the project is close to go-live and the SAP Build Apps backend projects are in use, we highly recommend switching to CAP.

What happens with my ongoing native mobile app projects?

We recommend using the mobile development kit and native SDKs, part of SAP Build offering / SAP Mobile services.

Are there any further features and roadmap items planned for SAP Build Apps?

No, there will be no additional features or data center support for SAP Build Apps going forward.

Will pricing change with the transition?

Yes, depending on the use case. For more information, please refer to the pricing page.

 

Next Steps

  • Learn more about the latest SAP Build offering
  • Get a 30 days access for a free trial to explore all SAP Build capabilities
  • Discover the SAP Build pricing model on our pricing page.
  • Further information mentioned in the help portal.

If you have any questions, concerns, or would like to discuss what this means for your specific setup, please don’t hesitate to reach out to your account manager or leave a comment below. We’re here to support you every step of the way.

entry.title: Unlock Enterprise-Scale Integration and Automation with SAP: A 5-Part Webcast Series
entry.content:
Running integration at enterprise scale requires more than connecting systems - it demands a unified strategy, consistent governance, and a strong operational foundation. To help organizations navigate this complex landscape, we’re excited to announce a deep-dive 5-part webcast series that shows how SAP Integration Suite and SAP Business Technology Platform can serve as a true enterprise-wide integration platform.

Whether you’re looking to simplify complexity, strengthen governance, or enable future-ready automation, this series provides a structured path from vision to sustainable execution. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect from each session:

 

1. Rethinking Integration: Building a Unified Enterprise-wide Platform

Integration starts with consolidation and unification. This session explores how organizations can position SAP Integration Suite as a central platform, connecting systems, processes, and data across the enterprise. Learn strategies to streamline your integration landscape and create a foundation for scalable, enterprise-wide operations.

Register here:  https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661068&sourcepage=register

 

2. From Vision to Execution: Building a Scalable Integration Strategy

Turning integration vision into reality requires a deliberate, structured approach. This session dives into the steps organizations can take to develop a scalable integration strategy that aligns business goals with technology execution. Discover best practices for governance, architecture, and execution that drive measurable value.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661111&sourcepage=register

 

3. Achieve Enterprise-grade Operations for SAP Integration Suite

Operational excellence is critical to sustaining integration at scale. In this session, we focus on how to strengthen governance, monitor performance, and build resilient processes with SAP Integration Suite. Learn how enterprise-grade operations ensure consistency, reliability, and scalability across your integration landscape.

Regiser here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259511&sessionid=1&key=905F0343E0BA503A283C97B3645BCB31&groupId=6667225&sourcepage=register

 

4. Drive Intelligent Business Transformation with Agentic Enterprise Automation

Intelligent business transformation requires more than isolated automation - it demands connected, adaptive processes powered by AI. Explore how SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Transformation Management enable agentic enterprise automation at scale. Learn how to design, govern, and measure AI agents that drive true process transformation, generating measurable outcomes across complex enterprise environments.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259684&sessionid=1&key=10935CA2537B96624DD065F7EA514C2F&groupId=6661382&sourcepage=register

 

5. Develop Robust Automations with SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite

Agentic enterprise automation only succeeds when systems, workflows, and applications are seamlessly connected. This session demonstrates how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite work together to enable end-to-end automation across SAP and non-SAP landscapes. See live demos, customer success stories, and practical examples showing how low-code development and enterprise-grade integration accelerate automation while maintaining control, visibility, and scalability.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5258498&sessionid=1&key=76AD4CD903C206A20A2CFFE8270CC25C&groupId=6661286&sourcepage=register

 

This series is designed for integration architects, IT leaders, and business transformation professionals who want to take their enterprise integration and automation initiatives to the next level. Join us to learn how SAP can help you simplify complexity, achieve operational excellence, and unlock intelligent, future-ready automation.

Save your spot 🙂

entry.title: Inside SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 2026: What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is almost here, and SAP Build at Sapphire is where customers and partners will be able to see how to easily create and deploy AI agents starting with simple language descriptions.  

For anyone tracking where AI agents and agentic development are headed in the enterprise, SAP Build will offer a chance to see it up close. Attendees will be able to experience how SAP Build helps teams build AI agents across SAP. This includes hands-on experience, live demos, sessions, and direct access to the teams building these capabilities. 

This year, the focus is on making agentic development real - moving from intent to working solution through a guided, end-to-end experience. 

 The hands-on experience: Build an agent with Joule Studio 

The biggest draw for SAP Build at Sapphire will be the hands-on experience on the show floor. 

Attendees can sit down at an open station and work through guided scenarios in a live system – building agents with Joule Studio, generating app logic from intent, and integrating with SAP data and workflows. This is a practical view at how agentic development works from start to deployment. 

This experience builds on the popular SAP Build Developer Garage at TechEd, which became a go-to stop for developers who wanted to get hands-on and see SAP Build in action. At Sapphire, we are bringing that energy back and making it bigger for a broader audience. 

Space will be limited, and access will be first-come, first-serve, so this is one stop worth making early. 

The sessions 

SAP Build sessions at Sapphire will focus on how teams are building AI agents, clean-core extensions and automation workflows. You’ll see how agentic development is evolving - from defining intent to generating and deploying working solutions - and how Joule is integrated across the development lifecycle. 

For anyone trying to separate AI hype from AI execution, these sessions are worth prioritizing. 

The booth 

The SAP Build and Agentic AI booths are where live walk-throughs and real conversations happen. 

Bring your own use cases, connect directly with SAP experts, and validate how SAP Build fits into your architecture. Whether you are building your first extension or scaling AI-driven workflows, the booth’s are designed for both strategic and practical discussions.  

Expect demos, conversations, and a chance to get closer to what SAP Build is showing at Sapphire. 

 

Why stop by SAP Build at SAP Sapphire 

AI agents are a major focus at Sapphire but SAP Build is where you can see how they are actually built and deployed in SAP environments. 

Get hands-on. Talk to the experts. See how agentic development works in practice. 

Plan your SAP Build stop at SAP Sapphire: 

  • Register for SAP Sapphire 
  • Browse SAP Build sessions 
  • Find the SAP Build booth (see the map onsite)

entry.title: Our 2026 Roadmap for Joule for Developers ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
Sapphire 2026 is approaching quickly and as usual it will bring lots of new announcements. So now is the right point in time to recap what has been announced already until Q2/2026 and prepare for the ABAP AI sessions.

The ABAP Platform AI (covering both SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA) transitions from a set of independent AI skills to full-scale Agentic AI. The roadmap focuses heavily on increasing developer productivity via agents in Joule for Developers ABAP (J4D ABAP) and the modernization of ABAP development tools.

The key benefits for customers and partners of the roadmap until Q2/2026 are:

  1. ABAP development tools with AI Agents in VS Code with ABAP MCP Server.
  2. ABAP AI for lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (i.e. 2021, 2022, 2023 and 2025).
  3. Simplified customer on-boarding of ABAP AI via SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration.
  4. Faster innovation with Side-by-Side consumption of SAP and 3rd party Agentic IDEs and LLMs
    (e.g. Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral…)
 

The biggest shift in 2026 will be the move toward Agentic AI, where the existing ABAP AI skills doesn't just explain, complete or generate code (e.g. RAP, CDS, Tests) independently but acts as an autonomous collaborator. This includes built-in ABAP MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. The introduction of VS Code as IDE can only be a start as the depth and breath of Eclipse which we achieved in so many years cannot be reached with one or two steps in VS Code. It'll be a journey which starts with first providing End-to-end UI services development with agents.

The new ABAP AI side-by-side architecture is designed to provide a scalable and future‑proof foundation for ABAP AI capabilities across heterogeneous SAP landscapes. It enables customers to consume ABAP AI services side‑by‑side, independent of the underlying S/4HANA release, ensuring access even for lower‑release Private Edition systems. 

A core benefit of the architecture is also its simplified enablement model through SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration, reducing on-boarding effort and ensuring consistent lifecycle management across environments. 
In addition, the architecture allows us to accelerate innovation speed by allowing to leverage SAP‑provided ABAP AI models alongside leading third‑party LLMs (e.g., Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral) more independently from releases. This flexibility ensures rapid adoption of new capabilities without dependency on individual release cycles.

If you missed the highlights we delivered in Q1/2026 have a look into what Olga Dolinskaja shared about ABAP AI ATC finding explain and custom code explain and proposals. Although this will be a focus also for our future roadmap we will also not stop investing into AI app generators like the Embedded Analytics model generator. 

 
Here you can learn more:
  1. SAP Joule for Developers (J4D) Expands to Private Cloud: Accelerating ABAP Innovation and Transformation
  2. Your Roadmap for getting started with ABAP AI
  3. ABAP AI Revolution Accelerates: The ABAP Developer Who Built Enterprise Apps in Minutes
  4. Joule for Developers and ABAP AI capabilities are coming to SAP S/4HANA Private Edition 2021, 2022, and 2023
  5. Public Roadmap: SAP Help / SAP Roadmap Explorer
  6. Help & Guides
    1. Step-by-step guide how to activate SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
    2. Joule for Developers (J4D) for S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup Guide
    3. SAP ABAP AI Discovery Center
    4. SAP BTP Discovery Center (Free Tier Services)
    5. Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
    6. YouTube playlist for Joule for Developers (J4D)
    7. Joule for Developers Help (J4D)
    8. Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud and SAP Joule for developers (RAP120) 
    9. ADT Eclipse AI Capabilities Help
    10. ABAP AI SDK Help
    11. ABAP-1 Help

More high-level insights about Joule for Developers/ABAP AI and ABAP platform can be found here . Here you find more on ABAP- 1,

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 26.3
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) is now available for all SAP Build and Mobile Services customers. You can download it from the SAP Software Center, or from the community download page.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has been updated to version 26.3.0 on the Apple App Store and the Google Play Store.

SAP MDK enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / Visual Studio Code) and run it natively on Mobile (iOS & Android).  

Below UI enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • Support Styling of Key Value Item Control
  • Contact Cell Control Enhancements
  • Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)
  • New ActionBar Caption Size Options
  • Support Required Field Indicator for Segmented FormCell Control
  • Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services
  • Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

Support Styling of Key Value Item Control

MDK now supports styling Key Value item control. A new object property Styles has been introduced with following sub-properties:

  • KeyValueItem: supports only background color to style entire item.
  • KeyName: supports to style key name.
  • Value: supports to style a value property.
//excerpt of an MDK page
"KeyAndValues": [{
        "Value": "{City}",
        "_Type": "KeyValue.Type.Item",
        "_Name": "KeyValue0",
        "KeyName": "City",
        "Visible": true,
        "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/UpdateEntitySuccessMessage.action",
        "LinkColor": "#0000FF",
        "Styles": {
            "KeyValueItem": "KeyValueItemBackground",
            "KeyName": "KeyNameStyle",
            "Value": "KeyValueStyle"
        }
    },

//excerpt of a .less file 
.KeyValueItemBackground {
    background-color: #FFA500;
}
.KeyNameStyle {
    font-color: #ADD8E6;
    text-decoration: underline;
}
.KeyValueStyle {
    font-color: green;
}

Note:  : Value  style will take priority over LinkColor, regardless of whether OnPress is defined or not.

 

Key Value Styling on iOS and Andrid devices

 For more information, refer to the Key Value Item documentation.

Contact Cell Control Enhancements

  • Support for Initials: The ContactCell component now supports displaying user initials, providing a more personalized and recognizable visual experience when profile pictures are unavailable. This enhancement makes it easier to scan and identify contacts by replacing generic placeholder icons with meaningful initials. Three new properties have been introduced to support this feature:
    • DetailImageText: A string property that displays text in the image container when no DetailImage is present. It supports a maximum of 2–4 characters, allowing you to manually specify custom text such as initials.
    • DetailImageIsCircular: A boolean property with a default value of true that controls the shape of the image container. When set to true, the image is displayed in a circular shape; when set to false, it is displayed in a rounded rectangular shape.
    • UseHeadlineForDetailImage: A boolean property with a default value of false. When set to true and no DetailImage is provided, the control automatically extracts and displays the first two initials from the Headline property in the image container. This property takes precedence over DetailImageText.
  • Support for BadgeImage Property:  A new property, BadgeImage, is now available on ContactCell. This property allows you to attach an additional indicator icon or image to the existing DetailImage or DetailImageText for each contact. For example, it can be used to highlight and identify primary contacts in a contact list. Additionally, the existing Styles property for ContactCellItem now supports a new subproperty named BadgeImage, which enables CSS styling for the indicator icon or image defined in the BadgeImage property.
//excerpt of .page
{
    "ContactCell": {
        "_Name": "ContactCellItem6",
        "BadgeImage": "sap-icon://bell",
        "DetailImageText": "SB",
        "DetailImageIsCircular": true,
        "Headline": "Sue Bay",
        "Subheadline": "Antioch, Illinois",
        "UseHeadlineForDetailImage": false,
        "Visible": true,
        "ActivityItems": [{
                "_Name": "ContactCellItem4ActivityItems0",
                "ActivityType": "Phone"
            }
        ],
        "Styles": {
            "DetailImage": "DetailImage",
            "BadgeImage": "BadgeImage"
        }
    }
},

//excerpt of Styles.less
.BadgeImage {
    color: #068831;
    background-color: #ffae00;
}
.DetailImage {
    color: #AD00FF;
    background-color: #ADD8E6;
}

Contact Cell Control on iOS and Android devices

For more information, refer to the Contact Cell documentation.

Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)

We have received feedback from customers about the MDK app restarting after taking a photo using the Attachment control, especially when the app is running on Android devices with lower RAM. This may be due to memory pressure on the device, which can cause the OS to kill the MDK app running on background after launching the camera.

To address this problem, we are providing support for using an in-app camera option. This option consumes less memory, and the camera opens directly inside the MDK app instead of switching to a separate camera application.

  • A new UseInAppCamera property is available in the Application.app file under the Settings object. It determines whether the in-app camera or the device's default camera will be used for the AttachmentFormCell control’s “Take Photo” option.
    • When set to true, the in-app camera will be used instead of launching the device’s native camera application.
//excerpt of Application.app
{
    "MainPage": "/MDK263/Pages/Main.page",
    "OnLaunch": [
        "/MDK263/Rules/Service/Initialize.js"
    ],
    "Settings": {
        "UseInAppCamera": true
    },
    "_Name": "MDK263"
}

In addition, we are introducing two new APIs to manage the in-app camera settings programmatically:

  • getUseInAppCamera 
    • Retrieves the current state of the in-app camera usage flag.
    • Returns the current value determining whether the in-app camera is enabled for FormCell Attachment controls.
  • setUseInAppCamera 
    • Enables or disables the in-app camera for FormCell Attachment controls
    • Changes take effect immediately for new AttachmentFormCell instances
    • For AttachmentFormCell controls that are already rendered on the current page, the new setting will only take effect after calling the control’s reset() API. Calling the reset() API will discard any existing attachments in the control.
export default function ChangeUseInAppCamera(context) {
  let currentValue = context.getUseInAppCamera();
  context.setUseInAppCamera(!currentValue);
  alert('UseInAppCamera set to ' + !currentValue);
  let currentPageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
  if (currentPageProxy) {
    if (currentPageProxy.getName() === 'FCMain') {
      let attachmentControlProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI('#Page:FCMain/#Control:FormCellAttachment0');
      if (attachmentControlProxy) {
        attachmentControlProxy.reset();
      }
    }
  }
}

New ActionBar Caption Size Options

The ActionBar now supports flexible caption sizing through the new PreferredCaptionSize property, which replaces the existing PrefersLargeCaption property.

  • Enhanced size control: Choose from small, medium, or large caption sizes instead of toggling between only two options.
    • iOS supports small and large caption size
    • Android supports small, medium, and large caption size.

Behavior Notes:        

  • When PreferredCaptionSize is set to "large", any defined Subhead will be ignored.
  • When setting CaptionAlignment (Android only) to “center”, the PreferCaptionSize automatically set to small.  
  • On iOS, large caption mode is not supported on pages with BottomNavigation, Tabs, SideDrawer, FormCellContainer, or FlexibleColumn controls.
  • If both PreferredCaptionSize and the deprecated PrefersLargeCaption property are specified, PreferredCaptionSize takes precedence.
//excerpt of an MDK page 
"ActionBar": {
        "Items": [
            {
                "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBarItem",
                "_Name": "ActionBarItem0",
                "Caption": "User Menu",
                "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/Application/UserMenuPopover.action"
            }
        ],
        "_Name": "ActionBar1",
        "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBar",
        "Caption": "MDK 26.3",
        "PreferredCaptionSize": "Medium"
    }
}

 

Support Required Field Indicator  for Segmented FormCell Control 

In MDK 25.9 release, we introduced support for required field indicators for some FormCell controls. In this release, we are extending this support to the Segmented FormCell, indicating that the field must be filled out.
Note: This feature does not enforce users to fill out the form cell; it only provides a visual indicator that the field is required.

//Excerpt from a .page
{
    "RequiredIndicator": true,
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl",
    "_Name": "PrioritySegmented",
    "Caption": "Priority",
    "ApportionsSegmentWidthsByContent": false,
    "Segments": [
        "Low",
        "Medium",
        "High"
    ]
}

Form Cell Segmented control on iOS and Android devices

 

Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services 

MDK now provides the ability for applications to upload the offline store (Both the main offline database (UDB) and the Request Queue database) to Mobile Services to assist with troubleshooting. This capability helps developers and support teams more effectively investigate and diagnose issues related to the offline store, reducing the time required to identify the root cause of problems reported by business users.
To facilitate this capability, a new MDK action has been introduced: Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore.

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "UploadOfflineDB"
    },
    "Service": "/MDK263/Services/SampleServiceV4.service",
    "EncryptionKey": "MDK123", // encryption key used during the upload process
    "Note": "My Test App" // Optional note attached to the uploaded database file
}

In order to use this feature, you will need to first enable offline store upload policy in SAP Mobile Services Admin UI for a given MDK app. Developers can download the uploaded files on their local machine and use ILODATA tool (link to doc) to analyze them.  (link how to use this tool). For more information, see this  documentation.

Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

MDK now supports onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration). Company administrators can push onboarding settings directly to managed devices through their MDM solution.

The MDK client retrieves MDK-defined configurations from a reserved root property named MDKSettings within the Managed Configuration. Currently, this root property only accepts the ConnectionSettings object. The supported properties, their functionality, and the required structure for ConnectionSettings remain the same as in the existing implementation.

While the iOS and Android versions differ syntactically due to their file formats, they share the same structural hierarchy. Note that the actual syntax may vary depending on the requirements of the specific MDM solution being used.

  • iOS: MDM solutions for iOS commonly use an XML format for complex configurations.
  • Android: MDM solutions for Android commonly use a JSON format for complex configurations.

Become an SAP MDK Explorer!
Do you have a requirement to build a mobile solution for your enterprise? Take your first steps today by following these tutorials to get started and learn more about the SAP Mobile development kit. We’re excited to see what you build—your feedback and ideas are invaluable!

Submit Improvement Requests
You can submit your improvement request (link) for Mobile development kit to the SAP Customer Influence site, a central place for all product improvement requests. You can browse all improvement requests, submit improvement requests, comment, vote, receive updates, and see who has voted at the SAP Customer Influence site.

entry.title: Building Custom Joule Skills via RFC: Guide for SAP S/4HANA and ECC Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s digital enterprise landscape, rapid access to critical business data can significantly impact operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. SAP’s AI-powered digital assistant, Joule, offers organizations the ability to interact with their enterprise systems through natural language conversations. By extending Joule with custom skills, businesses can create tailored experiences that bring real-time data directly to their teams—without the need for complex navigation through traditional ERP interfaces.

This article provides a comprehensive technical guide for building custom Joule skills that connect to SAP S/4HANA or ECC systems via RFC (Remote Function Call). We’ll explore a practical use case and walk through the complete configuration process.

Business Scenario

Consider a common challenge faced by sales and operations teams: A customer inquires about the manufacturing progress of their order. Traditionally, the specialist would need to log into the ERP system, navigate through multiple screens, and manually locate the production order details. This process is time-consuming and interrupts the flow of customer communication.

With a custom Joule skill, this same specialist can simply ask Joule in natural language—"What’s the status of production order 12345?"—and receive immediate, formatted information directly within SAP Build Workzone. This streamlined approach enhances productivity and improves response times to customer inquiries.

 

 

Solution Architecture

The technical architecture comprises several integrated SAP components:

  • SAP Build Workzone: The user interface where specialists interact with Joule
  • Joule Studio: The development environment for designing and building custom AI skills
  • SAP Build: Used to create Action Projects that expose backend functionality
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Hosts destination configurations and orchestrates connections
  • SAP Cloud Connector: Establishes secure RFC connections between BTP and on-premise or private cloud systems
  • SAP S/4HANA / ECC: The backend ERP system containing production order data

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Establish RFC Connectivity

The foundation of this solution is a secure connection between your cloud and on-premise environments.

Build RFC Connection with SAP Cloud Connector:
Configure the SAP Cloud Connector to create a secure tunnel between your BTP environment and your S/4HANA or ECC system. This ensures that backend functions can be accessed securely without exposing your entire system to the internet.

 

Setup Destination in BTP Cockpit:
Create a destination in your BTP subaccount that references the RFC connection established through the Cloud Connector. This destination will be consumed by SAP Build to enable communication with your backend system.

 

Step 2: Create a BAPI/RFC Action Project

With connectivity established, the next step is to expose the specific backend functionality that Joule will use.

Create the Action Project:
Navigate to the SAP Build cockpit and create a new BAPI/RFC Action project. Search for the BAPI that retrieves production order information—for example, BAPIs related to production order management.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Test the Connection:
After creating the action project, test the connection to verify that data can be successfully retrieved from your ERP system. By default, these actions operate in READ ONLY mode to protect your business data from unintended modifications.

 

 

Optional Verification with SE37:
For deeper technical validation, you can use transaction code SE37 in your S/4HANA or ECC system to directly test the BAPI function with various parameters. This helps you understand the data structures and responses before integrating them into your Joule skill.

 

 

Step 3: Design Skills in Joule Studio

This is where you design the conversational experience and connect it to your backend data.

Skill 1: Get Production Orders

Each skill consists of three major components:

  1. Skill Description: Define the purpose of the skill and the natural language triggers that will activate it when users interact with Joule.
  2. BAPI Action: Link the skill to the BAPI/RFC Action project you created in SAP Build, establishing the connection between the conversational interface and your backend data.
  3. Send Message: Design the end-user output using the Message Configuration editor. This allows you to transform raw BAPI data into formatted, interactive cards that present information clearly and professionally.

 

 

 

Skill 2: Get Production Order Detail

Following the same pattern, create a second skill that provides detailed information about a specific production order. This allows users to drill down from a list view to detailed information through natural conversation.

 

 

 

Step 4: Deploy and Enable in Workzone

The final phase makes your custom skills accessible to end users.

Create a Shared Environment:
In Joule Studio, create a Shared environment that will serve as the runtime for your deployed skills.

 

 

Deploy Your Joule Project:
Deploy your Joule project to the Shared environment. Once deployed, verify that the project appears under Custom Capabilities.

 

 

Configure Joule in SAP Build Workzone:
Set up the integration between Joule and your SAP Build Workzone site. After completing the integration configuration, enable Joule within your specific Workzone site.

 

 

Test Your Custom Capabilities:
Access your Workzone site, open the Joule client, and test your new custom AI capabilities by asking questions in natural language about production orders.

 

 

Benefits and Outcomes

By implementing custom Joule skills, organizations can achieve:

  • Increased Productivity: Eliminate time-consuming navigation through complex ERP interfaces
  • Improved Customer Service: Enable faster, more accurate responses to customer inquiries
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provide intuitive, conversational access to critical business data
  • Secure Data Access: Maintain enterprise-grade security while democratizing data access
  • Reduced Training Requirements: Lower the learning curve for accessing ERP information

Conclusion

Building custom Joule skills via RFC represents a powerful approach to modernizing how users interact with SAP systems. By combining the conversational AI capabilities of Joule with the deep business logic in S/4HANA or ECC, organizations can create intelligent, context-aware experiences that transform daily operations.

The technical implementation, while requiring careful configuration across multiple components, provides a robust and secure foundation for extending SAP’s AI capabilities to meet specific business needs. As organizations continue their digital transformation journeys, solutions like these demonstrate how AI can be practically applied to solve real business challenges.

Additional Resources

  • Build Your First Joule Skill in Joule Studio
  • Joule Studio CodeJam
  • Build custom Joule skills for SAP and non-SAP systems using Joule Studio

entry.title: VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026: Champions Crowned at SYNC DemoJam
entry.content:
The Stage is Set
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 reached its spectacular grand finale on the main stage of VNSG SYNC 2026 – the annual VNSG and SAP Netherlands year event. In front of a packed keynote audience on 12 March, three finalist teams had exactly five minutes to demonstrate the business impact of their SAP BTP-powered innovations. The audience then cast their votes – and the results were electric.

From Challenge to Champion: The Journey
The hackathon launched in late 2025 when VNSG, SAP BTP Product Management and SAP NL Presales invited Dutch SAP customers and partners to submit real-life business challenges. Six teams were selected and, after a structured enablement phase, spent weeks co-building solutions with dedicated coaches. In February 2026, all six teams pitched to a jury panel, which selected three finalists for the grand DemoJam finale at SYNC. The three finalists stood out for their clear business relevance, technical ambition and the real-world potential to take their prototypes to production.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Champion: Promocean + SOA People — Purchase Order Control Center
Luc van Elk and Thomas Langhout from Promocean, supported by SOA People, took the audience vote and claimed the top spot. Their solution – the Purchase Order Control Center – puts SAP Business AI at the heart of procurement operations.
By connecting AI directly to SAP ECC, the team demonstrated how intelligent automation can dramatically reduce manual effort, accelerate purchase order processing, and surface exceptions before they become costly problems. A showcase of how AI on SAP BTP can deliver operational excellence in one of the most common – and pain-prone – business processes in any enterprise.

Runner-up: Ecotone + Expertum — Integration Suite Management
Arjan de Mol and Bart van de Kamp from Ecotone, partnered with Expertum Consulting, earned second place with a solution that tackles a pain many integration teams know all too well: managing and controlling large numbers of iFlows across landscapes. Their Integration Suite Management solution brings Joule-powered automation to iFlow lifecycle management – enabling operators to undeploy and deploy groups of iFlows intelligently. This turns a time-consuming, error-prone manual process into a governed, AI-assisted workflow, giving integration teams transparency, stability and control. 

Third Place: NTT DATA Business Solutions — ProTrack
Laurens Steffers and Jorrit van der Sanden from NTT DATA Business Solutions presented ProTrack – a solution delivering real-time insight for machines on the production floor. Built on SAP BTP, ProTrack addresses a critical operational need in manufacturing: knowing what is happening on your shop floor right now, not minutes later. By streaming machine data into an SAP BTP-powered dashboard, teams can react faster to exceptions, reduce downtime and optimize throughput.

 

Beyond the Podium: A Strong Field
The jury had a hard job. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS), and Simac IT & BSC also built
impressive solutions that demonstrated real business value on SAP BTP. Every team that reached the jury pitch proved
that you can go from business challenge to working prototype in a short time - when you combine the right platform
with the right people. A sincere congratulations to all six participating teams !!

Behind the Scenes
Want to experience the atmosphere of the DemoJam from backstage? Watch the behind-the-scenes video - including a
live demo of how SAP Business AI is changing the way people interact with SAP, presented by Jason Enti and Ronald
Schippers: Behind the scenes at the VNSG SAP BTP DemoJam SYNC 2026

The People Who Made it Happen
A hackathon like this does not happen by itself. A huge thank you to everyone who contributed:
Presented at SYNC by: Twan van den Broek and Irene Rompa
Team coaches: Jan Penninkhof , Alex van den Hoek and Twan van den Broek
Jury: Ronald Schippersm Steven Nuyts, Steffen Schad, Jonathan Freeland
Made possible by: Mientje Paais, Olaf Winkler, Judy Bui and Niklas Siemer

What This Proves
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 is proof that innovation does not require a blank slate or a greenfield project. It requires the right platform, the right people and a real problem worth solving.
SAP BTP – combining SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Business AI and cloud-native runtimes – gives teams everything they need to move from prototype to production at speed.
The finalist solutions were not demos for the sake of demos. They addressed real procurement pain, real integration
complexity and real manufacturing visibility gaps. And they were built in weeks, not months.

What's Next?
The conversation does not end on the SYNC stage. All teams are encouraged to work with their coaches to plan production rollouts and turn hackathon prototypes into enterprise solutions.

For the broader SAP Community : Stay tuned for the next edition of the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon. Bring your own
real-life business challenge and co-build with partners and SAP experts.
And who knows - your solution could be on the SYNC main stage in 2027.

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite SAP Business AI 

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

Related reading:
When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact – VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon in a DemoJam

entry.title: Joule for Developers ABAP AI Sapphire and ASUG 2026 Sessions
entry.content:
In case you do not have planned your Sapphire Orlando and ASUG agenda here are my recommendations if you want to learn about the latest innovations from ABAP Platform and Joule for developers (J4D) ABAP AI capabilities:

1. Introduction to ABAP Cloud supported by generative AI | BTP1429: Get an overview on ABAP Cloud and recent innovations, get insights on Clean Core and see how generative AI boosts your adoption of ABAP Cloud conducted by Alexander Rother (VP, Head of ABAP Platform Product Management).

2. Build smarter: Agentic ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code | BTP2573: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform) will show how ABAP agents orchestrate in VS Code via the ABAP MCP-server tasks, speed up coding, and boost productivity across orchestrators from various partners.

3. Agentic ABAP with SAP Joule for Developers: Build faster, migrate smarter | JOU1428: Simona Marincei (Head of AI - SAP ABAP Platform) and Nora Klemp (Senior Product Specialist) will explain how agentic ABAP accelerates SAP S/4HANA migrations and streamline ABAP Cloud development across modern and legacy environments.

4. Customer Influence and feedback: The product road map for ABAP Cloud | BTP1603: Learn from Alexander Rother (VP, Head of ABAP Platform Product Management) how to give feedback to the ABAP product roadmap with the Customer Influence site which allows submitting, tracking, and prioritizing ideas.

5. Modernize classic extensions for clean core in SAP Cloud ERP Private | BTP1426: Volker Drees (Product Expert, ABAP Cloud, SAP SE) will explain clean core extensibility practice in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Learn how to govern clean core development using the ABAP test cockpit and how to use the ABAP Cloud development model and clean core extensibility principles to modernize your custom on-stack extensions.

 

The full Sapphire Orlando and ASUG 2026 (11.05.2026 - 13.05.2026) agenda can be found here...
...and here is the agenda for Sapphire Madrid (19.05.2026 - 21.05.26).

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP - Q1 2026 Innobytes
entry.content:
This first quarter has set the tone for 2026, bringing in new ideas and innovations all around Data, Joule and more. Let’s dive into some of the highlights Q1 has brought us.

 

 

AI & Data Foundation Highlight

Discovery and Data Agent in SAP HANA Cloud

The new Discovery and Data Agents are lowering learning barriers for developers and data users who just need answers. These new agents make it easier to explore, analyze, and act on data without needing deep knowledge of the underlying artifacts.

To see this demo in action, watch the video (starting at 0:11).

Learn more

  • Discovery & Data Agent on SAP Community
  • Register for the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents - Beta Program

 

Integration Highlight

Fresh Connections in SAP Integration Suite

New adapters for SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP help transform your integration landscape, breaking down data silos, streamlining operations, and allow rapid adoption of new capabilities to innovate faster. 

The latest release offers:

  • The BambooHR adapter enables receiving and processing employee lifecycle data effectively.
  • The Microsoft Outlook Sender and Receiver adapter allows seamless integration for calendars, mail, attachments, and messaging directly into your mission-critical workflows. 

For Edge Integration Cella:

  • The File adapter, enabling secure file exchange with NFS systems
  • The OFTP2 adapter for compliant B2B connections to securely exchange messages from business partners. 

See the new integration content (starting at 2:14).

Learn more:

  • Integration Adapter
  • SAP Integration Suite on SAP Community

 

Agent & Application Development Highlights

API based Action Error Handling

In real-world automation, failures are inevitable. What matters is how the system handles them. This innovation brings precision and predictability to API error handling in SAP Build Process Automation on SAP BTP. When configuring an API-based Action step, enable error handling and define specific errors, handling authentication failures precisely. You can create generic categories, such as 4XX or 5XX, handling broader client or server issues.

The inability to define category overlap eliminates ambiguity, and deterministic runtime behavior is guaranteed. When an error occurs, precedence is strictly enforced:

  • Specific errors first
  • Generic category second
  • Undefined fallback last

At the Action level, you can configure retries that define the number of attempts and the delay between them. Afterwards, the error handling logic is executed. Every stage is fully logged in Monitoring, ensuring complete traceability and turning error handling from a black box into a controlled, observable workflow decision.

Watch the demo (starting at 3:35).

 

Conversational Work in SAP Build Work Zone 

There is now a powerful new way to work in SAP Build Work Zone. Save time by simply asking Joule what you need instead of clicking through multiple pages.

In the demo (starting at 5:33), a new project workspace for a cross-functional initiative is set up through a short conversation with Joule, enabling easy navigation, no technical steps, and only one screen. SAP Build Work Zone becomes more conversational, more intuitive, and more aligned with how people actually work.

Learn more:

  • Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

 

Core Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot and Joule: Unlocking AI-Powered Ops Agents

The SAP Automation Pilot and Joule turn repeatable operational tasks into intelligent, agent-driven capabilities on SAP BTP.

By starting with a real operational process like a S/4HANA health check, use Automation Pilot’s low-code/no-code interface. Here, the necessary steps, inputs and validations can be collected and grouped into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server. The MCP Server exposes these automations as governed, reusable tools that can be safely consumed by AI agents without writing custom APIs.

Once published, connect it to Joule Studio to build a Joule Ops Agent that understands when and how to invoke these tools. The Ops Agent can now detect issues or fetch system status, triggering the right automation behind the scenes.

The DevOps or Admin team can simply ask the Ops Agent in Joule to ‘check my HANA Cloud and App instances health and remediate common issues’ by using the MCP Server to execute these steps securely, consistently, and audibly.

For a demo, watch the video (starting at 7:35).

Learn more:

  • Blog Post: From Automation to Agentic Ops
  • Blog Post: Meet your Ops Agents

 

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU, and we would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • Unlocking SAP BTP: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-Guided Implementation Workshop Series
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud is a fully managed, in-memory cloud database service as part of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It delivers high-speed processing and real-time analytics, which is especially valuable for data-intensive applications. The platform supports multiple data models, including relational, graph, spatial, JSON, and document store, allowing developers to work with different types of data in one environment. It also includes built-in data virtualization and integration capabilities, enabling organizations to access and combine data from SAP and non-SAP sources.

SAP HANA Cloud provides a comprehensive environment for building intelligent, data-driven solutions. It supports advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities directly within the database, which reduces data movement and accelerates insight generation. Developers can use familiar tools and languages such as SQL, Java, Node.js, and Python to create applications and services.
The platform also includes data lake functionality for storing and processing large volumes of less-frequently accessed data at a lower cost, while still allowing seamless querying alongside in-memory data. Its multi-tier storage approach helps organizations balance performance and cost by automatically managing where data resides. Overall, SAP HANA Cloud enables organizations to modernize their data landscape, support real-time decision-making, and drive digital transformation initiatives.

 

🚀 Empower Your Data Journey: Introducing Our New SAP HANA Cloud Expert Guided Implementation Series 

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, speed of transformation is a must. Businesses across the globe are evolving to make faster, smarter, and more connected decisions by building intelligent, data-driven solutions using advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities. To support this evolution, we’re excited to announce our renewed series of Expert-Guided Implementation workshops (EGI) from SAP Enterprise Support designed to help you upskill, modernize, and future-proof your business processes. Whether you're looking to modernize legacy systems, harness the power of cloud data, or unlock real-time insights through analytics, this workshop series offers targeted enablement for every stage of your development. 

*The following EGI sessions will run throughout the year, with quarterly updates to the schedule. 
Each session is limited to 30 individual customer participants.  
**Registration links below direct to SAP Learning content for SAP Enterprise Support, which requires a one-time registration with a valid S-user.

Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud

In this live EGI session we will guide you through the SAP HANA Cloud capabilities as DBaaS, demonstrate the key features available in the latest quarterly release of the tool and show the benefits of using the administration tools for DB Monitoring, security and configuration. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP HANA Cloud
  • Overview of SAP BTP
  • Introduction and usage of Administration tools
    • SAP HANA Cloud Central
    • SAP HANA Cockpit
    • SAP HANA Database Explorer
  • Managing, Monitoring and Alert Notification in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Workload Management:
    • Managing Tables
    • HANA NSE
  • SAP HANA HDI Overview
  • Introduction and usage of Cloud Foundry CLI 
  • Security Administration and User management

Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Modeling

In this live EGI session, we will guide you through the process of modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of calculation view usage and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio. Also, we showcase the core capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud for calculation view graphical modelling and provide help on about SQL Script procedures and implementing functions using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Usage of SAP Business Application Studio and Administration tools
  • Deploy SAP Business Application Studio
  • Introduction and modeling Calculation views
  •  Modeling with SAP Business Application Studio and using Database Explorer
  • Modeling features
  • Generating restricted and calculated columns
  • SQL Script usage
  • Performance Analysis, Debug mode & SQL Analyser
  • Overview of Best Practices

 Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph

In this live EGI session, we empower customers with the knowledge of SAP HANA Cloud multiple model programming using the latest concepts including vector engine, knowledge graph engine and retrieval augmented generation. After the completion of the workshop, customers will have the knowledge of multiple model engines and how to incorporate artificial intelligence and retrieval augmented generation capabilities.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understand SAP HANA Cloud architecture and deployment options
  • Introduction to multiple model programming
  • Data Tiering considerations for multiple model programming
  • Overview of SAP Business Application Studio
  • Spatial data concepts in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understanding spatial features and functions
  • Spatial data modeling and processing
  • Graph processing in SAP HANA Cloud
  • JSON document store in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Automated machine learning (AutoML) in SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Cloud vector database
  • Capabilities and benefits of multiple model processing with vectors
  • SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graph engine
  • Knowledge graph and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
  • Real-world applications for Retrieval Augmented Generation

Register Here* 

 

Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule)

In this live EGI session we will empower you with the knowledge of cloud application programming development in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of CAP CDS development and design in SAP HANA Cloud and understand how to build and deploying cloud application projects from scratch and with reduced costs utilizing pro-code development using SAP Build Code with Joule. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda:

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Introduction to SAP Build Code and Prompt Engineering
  • CAP Architecture and SAP Business Application Studio features
  • Build and deploy a CAP project using SAP Business Application Studio
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud using CAP CDS
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Optimization
  • Building Application using SAP Build Code and Joule
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Management and Administration

Register Here* 

Learning Outcomes from the EGI Series:

  • Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud:
    Master SAP HANA Cloud configuration, performance monitoring and secure user management using standard administration tools.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Modeling:
    Develop Calculation views and SQL Script procedures within SAP Business Application Studio.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph:
    Implement advanced Vector Engine, Knowledge Graph, and Spatial Data capabilities to power Generative AI and Retrieval Augmented Generation scenarios
  • Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule):
    Build and deploy end-to-end CAP applications efficiently using SAP Build Code and Joule


Who Should Attend? 

This workshop series is ideal for developers, modelers, business analysts, system administrators, database administrators and SAP customers looking to transform their business processes. Whether you're just getting started or want to go deeper into SAP's modern toolset, there’s a session tailored for your needs. 

 

GET MORE CONTENT LIKE THIS!

Within the group, explore the Data & Analytics Value Map to connect with your Foundational Support team. Simply click “Join Group”, and once inside, you can even request a call with a topic expert to get personalized guidance and enablement support.
